title: Fix rendering bugs and replace fonts/icons with LimeZu assets
description: Fix 4 rendering bugs from TMX v2 migration and deliver 2 new features: canvas font labels and LimeZu UI status icons.

dod.criterion: Characters are clickable — hit detection matches visual sprite size
Character names visible above sprites using canvas font
Status icons use LimeZu UI sprite sheet assets
FURNITURE_TOP matches latest TMX data
npx tsc --noEmit passes

scope_summary: 6 ordered tasks: (1) re-sync TMX tile data, (2) fix hit detection scaling, (3) fix name label positioning + replace bitmap font with canvas font, (4) copy LimeZu UI assets and replace status/interaction icons.
sequencing_rationale: TMX sync first to establish correct tile data. Hit detection next (independent fix). Label positioning and font replacement together (same code area). LimeZu icons last (builds on stable renderer).

tasks.title: Re-sync FURNITURE_TOP with latest TMX data
tasks.user_scenario: The office renders with the latest tile layout matching what the CEO designed in Tiled.
tasks.scope: Copy the FURNITURE_TOP array from the generated file (src/components/game/generated/office-tmx-data.ts) into office-layout.ts. The TMX parser has already been re-run and the generated file is up to date. Only FURNITURE_TOP has real changes (rows 7-9 and 12-13 — animated bathroom tiles moved). Other layers are identical.

dod.criterion: FURNITURE_TOP in office-layout.ts matches FURNITURE_TOP in generated/office-tmx-data.ts exactly
npx tsc --noEmit passes

tasks.title: Scale character hit detection by CHARACTER_SPRITE_SCALE
tasks.user_scenario: Clicking anywhere on a character's visible sprite selects them — not just the bottom half.
tasks.scope: In officeState.ts getCharacterAt() (line ~1458), the hit box uses CHARACTER_HIT_HALF_WIDTH=24 and CHARACTER_HIT_HEIGHT=48 which match the unscaled sprite. But characters are rendered at CHARACTER_SPRITE_SCALE (3×), so sprites appear 48×96px. Multiply the hit dimensions by CHARACTER_SPRITE_SCALE so the clickable area matches the visual. Import CHARACTER_SPRITE_SCALE from constants.ts. Update the constants CHARACTER_HIT_HALF_WIDTH and CHARACTER_HIT_HEIGHT if needed, or apply the scale factor in the hit test logic.

dod.criterion: getCharacterAt hit box covers the full visual sprite area (scaled by CHARACTER_SPRITE_SCALE)
CHARACTER_SPRITE_SCALE is imported and used in officeState.ts hit detection
npx tsc --noEmit passes

tasks.title: Fix name label positioning and replace bitmap font with canvas font
tasks.user_scenario: Character names appear clearly above each sprite in a clean, readable font.
tasks.scope: Two changes: (1) Fix NAME_LABEL_VERTICAL_OFFSET_PX in constants.ts — current value 84 is too high. The character sprite is 32px tall × 3 scale = 96px rendered height. Label should be ~8-12px above the sprite top, so offset should be approximately 96 + 10 = 106 from the character's anchor (bottom). Adjust until labels sit just above the character head. Also fix BUBBLE_VERTICAL_OFFSET_PX (currently 72) to match. (2) Replace renderBitmapText() calls in renderer.ts with ctx.font/ctx.fillText. Use a clean sans-serif font like '10px "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif' scaled by zoom. Remove the bitmapFont.ts import and switch to native canvas text. Update IDENTITY_PLATE_HEIGHT to match the new font metrics.

dod.criterion: Name labels are visible above character sprites at default zoom
Labels use ctx.font/ctx.fillText instead of renderBitmapText
bitmapFont.ts is no longer imported by renderer.ts
IDENTITY_PLATE_HEIGHT and NAME_LABEL_VERTICAL_OFFSET_PX values are recalculated for canvas font
npx tsc --noEmit passes

tasks.title: Replace status/interaction icons with LimeZu UI sprite sheet
tasks.user_scenario: Status bubbles above characters show polished LimeZu speech-bubble icons instead of hand-drawn pixel art.
tasks.scope: Copy UI_48x48.png from /Users/yangyang/Downloads/moderninteriors-win/4_User_Interface_Elements/UI_48x48.png to public/assets/office/UI_48x48.png. Create a new sprite loading module that slices the 48x48 sprite sheet into individual icon frames. The sprite sheet is a grid of 48×48 icons. Map status types to icons: working→gear icon (row 0, col ~6), idle→zzz/sleep icon (row 3, col ~10), planning→thinking dots, reviewing→checkmark, brainstorming→lightbulb, auditing→magnifying glass. Replace the hand-drawn SpriteData arrays in statusIcons.ts and interactionIcons.ts with image-based icons loaded from the sprite sheet. Update renderer.ts to render these as Image elements via drawImage instead of getCachedSprite from SpriteData arrays. Also copy relevant animated GIFs (UI_thinking_emote_dots_48x48.gif, UI_timer_green_to_green_48x48.gif) to public/assets/office/ for potential animated icon use.

dod.criterion: UI_48x48.png exists in public/assets/office/
Status icons render from the LimeZu sprite sheet instead of hand-drawn pixel art
At least 4 status types mapped to LimeZu icons (working, idle, planning, reviewing)
Icons appear in speech-bubble style above character name plates
npx tsc --noEmit passes
